name: D+Q first-in-human idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis open-label pilot
summary: Two-center open-label first-in-human pilot of intermittent dasatinib plus quercetin in idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis, designed primarily for feasibility with secondary safety and exploratory functional outcomes.
population: Fourteen participants with stable idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis receiving intermittent D+Q over three weeks.

trial_details.watch_status_reason: Publication PMID 30616998 already captures the study results; the no-posted-results state is registry-maintenance context only.
trial_details.horizon_note: Published first-in-human disease-specific pilot; ClinicalTrials.gov had no posted registry results in the June 11, 2026 API query.
trial_details.why_it_matters: This is the first human D+Q feasibility and physical-function signal, but its open-label design prevents efficacy attribution.
